calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR SHORT CAUSE
Off Calendar Nov-26-2007 Assigned To Trial Court

COURT TRIAL
Off Calendar Nov-30-2007 Judgment Entered
MINI MINUTES:
This matter came regularly on calendar this day, by assignment from the the Presiding Judge Dept. for Court Trial/Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 218, Commissioner Sue M. Kaplan, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert, CSR: Leticia Perez.
Atty. David J. Cook appearing for the Plaintiff.
Plaintiff's Witness: Odst Logan of the Pacific Gas & Electric Company, was sworn and direct examined.
No appearance by the Defendant: Tam Yan Pak;
The following exhibits were presented:
Exhibit 1- Adjustment Order--------------------ID/EVID.
Exhibit 2- Letter of Jan. 21, 2005 ---------ID/EVID.
Exhibit 3- Letter of February 5, 2005 ------ID/EVID
Exhibit 4- Business Card of Hongkong Seafood Restuarant ------ID/EVID
Exhibit 5- Assignment of Claim
Exhibit 6-License Query System from the Dept. of Alcohol Beverage

After hearing the testimony and evidence presented, the Court rules in favor of the Plaintiff M. Snow and against the Defendants Tam Yan Pak and Hongkong Seafood Restaurant, Inc. as follows:
Principal - $ 72,320.30 plus pre-judgment interest of ten percent from September 21, 2006 until the entry of judgment and cost (upon filing of memorandum of cost).
Counsel stipulated for the return of the Exhibits in open court, and to prepare Judgment for signature.

Nov. 30, 2007
Judgment signed by Comm. Kaplan, see scanned doc. for total amount and details of judgment.

MASTER CALENDAR SHORT CAUSE
Off Calendar Aug-01-2007 Set For Trial On Nov-26-07 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Ntc And Motion To Compel Response To Discovery
Off Calendar Sep-27-2007 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-17-2007 Set For Trial On Oct-15-07 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default
Off Calendar Jun-27-2007 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES- ATTY. PHILIP T. KILDUFF, TEL. NO. 673-0368 SPECIALLY APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. SZE-HANG LO, TEL. NO. (510)228-5388 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT AND MOVING PARTY.

RULING - A R G U E D ; THE COURT ADOPTED IT'S TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS - DEFENDANT YAN TAM'S Motion To Set Aside Default IS DENIED. DEFENDANT HAS FAILED TO SHOW ANY EXCUSABLE NEGLECT AND DEFENDANT HAS FAILED TO ATTACH A RESPONSIVE PLEADING AS REQUIRED BY C C P SECTION 473 (B). PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen CSR # 8189 =301/PJB/JF
